subject: Mathematics
title: Omar Khayyam and Cubic Equations
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Omar Khayyam and Cubic Equations.

hook.text: Omar Khayyam is famous for poetry, but he was an even greater mathematician.

buildup.text: In the 11th century, he classified and solved cubic equations using geometric methods.

discovery.text: He intersected conic sections—parabolas and circles—to find solutions no algebra could reach.

twist.text: He also reformed the Persian calendar. His version was more accurate than the Gregorian.

climax.text: Europe wouldn't solve cubics algebraically until Cardano's work 500 years later.

punchline.text: The poet who solved equations geometry couldn't touch.

quiz.question: How did Omar Khayyam solve cubic equations?
options: By trial and error with large numbers
